mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% in Oct 2010 indicates post-crisis labor slack
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero signals extreme monetary accommodation, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives capital allocation toward equities as alternative to near-zero cash returns, producing sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% in Oct 2010 reflects zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand and inventory replenishment cycles, which accelerate corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 373,103M in Oct 2010 reflects post-recession recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with declining unemployment indicates supply-side capacity utilization and labor productivity gains, which support profit margin expansion and positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.19 in Sep 2010 shows manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year regressions, or joint signal produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals demand weakness and future consumption contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 610K in Sep 2010 remains depressed post-crisis
regime_hint: housing-led demand weakness or recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% annually combined with positive real GDP growth signals monetary policy lag risk, which increases probability of future rate hikes and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.37 in Sep 2010 shows modest inflation
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with inflation acceleration risk
failure_condition: Interaction term is not significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 150K per month while unemployment remains elevated above 8%, signaling labor market slack absorption and future wage pressure, which increases corporate cost expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,462K and UNRATE at 9.6% in Oct 2010
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth rate decelerates below 2% annualized while retail sales growth stalls, signaling demand-side momentum loss and recession risk, which triggers equity risk-off positioning and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,260.7B in Q2 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery pace
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration or pre-recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
